stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Chinese ship hit on way in to Odesa port, says Zelenskyy
stories.summary: Civilian vessels under Russian attack in Black Sea; Kyiv condemns Belarus nuclear drills as threat to Nato and global security. What we know on day 1,546 Continue reading...

stories.title: Trump dismisses $10bn suit against IRS and creates $1.7bn ‘anti-weaponization’ fund
stories.summary: Democrats criticize deal as a slush fund that ‘funnels taxpayer dollars’ to president’s political allies Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The justice department announced on Monday it was creating a loosely controlled and secretive $1.776bn fund to compensate Donald Trump allies as part of an agreement in which Trump and his sons dropped a $10bn longshot lawsuit against the IRS. The money, which critics said was essentially a slush fund , will be overseen by five commissioners –

stories.title: Xi prepares to welcome Putin to China four days after hosting Trump
stories.summary: Chinese state media say Beijing emerging as ‘focal point of global diplomacy’ with Russian leader arriving on Tuesday Xi Jinping and Vladimir Putin exchanged “congratulatory letters” on Sunday ahead of the Russian president’s visit to Beijing this week, four days after Donald Trump left China after a high-stakes summit . Xi said bilateral cooperation between Russia and China had “continuously deepened and solidified”, Chinese state media reported, with this year marking the 30th anniversary of t

stories.title: Magyar Péter: Az igazságügyi miniszter nemlegesen terjesztette fel a Sándor-palotába K. Endre kegyelmi kérvényét
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök közölte, hogy kedden délelőtt nyilvánosságra hozzák a kegyelmi ügy egyik dossziéját.

stories.title: Magyar Péter szerint kötelező lesz megjelenni a parlamenti vizsgálóbizottság előtt
stories.summary: A kormányfő azt mondta, ha valaki nem jelenik meg, először bírságot kap, majd a rendőrség fogja bevezetni.

stories.title: Magyar Péter több miniszterrel, köztük Orbán Anitával utazik Lengyelországba és Bécsbe
stories.summary: Magyar Péter már áprilisban bejelentette, hogy a visegrádi négyek szövetségét megerősítené és kibővítené, az első útja ennek jegyében Lengyelországba fog vezetni.

stories.title: Havasi Bertalan szerint Orbán Viktornak kell megújulnia, nem a Fidesznek
stories.summary: Nem illeszkedik bele a miniszterelnök programjába – válaszolta arra Havasi Bertalan, hogy miért nem kapott többször lehetőséget a független sajtó arra, hogy Orbán Viktort kérdezhesse.

stories.title: A kormány már tárgyal az Európai Bizottsággal, de feltételt szabott Ukrajna uniós csatlakozásához
stories.summary: Magyar Péter kész Beregszászon találkozni Volodimir Zelenszkijjel, ha rendeződik az ukrajnai magyar kisebbség helyzete.

stories.title: Magyar Péter a nyár második felétől újra országjárásra indulhat
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök erről a köztársasági jogkörök bővítésével összefüggésben beszélt.

stories.title: Az önkormányzatoknak ki kell fizetniük a szolidaritási adót
stories.summary: Magyar Péter a kormányülés után mondta, hogy „jogos elvárása a kormánynak és az országnak, hogy mindenki teljesítse a jogi kötelezettségét”, és felajánlotta Budapest főpolgármesterének, hogy segít visszafogni a költségeket.

stories.title: Orbán Anita: Hazarendelem Magyarország varsói nagykövetét
stories.summary: A külügyminiszter szerint „a megújuló külpolitikai irányvonalat egy új személy tudja majd hitelesen és következetesen képviselni a következő időszakban.”

stories.title: Jönnek a kegyelmi ügy részletei, mennek a vármegyék – a kormányülés utáni sajtótájékoztató röviden
stories.summary: A maratoni sajtótájékoztatón szó volt a miniszterelnök lengyelországi és ausztriai útjáról, valamint arról is, hogy az új kormány rengeteg mindent felül fog vizsgálni.

stories.title: A streamingnek sem sikerült megölnie Magyarország legnagyobb lemezvásárát
stories.summary: A Lemezpiac a Pecsában született tradíciót viszi tovább, egyre csak növekvő látogatottsággal. Ott voltunk a születésnapján.

stories.title: Magyar: Case files of clemency case to be published
stories.summary: Speaking at a press conference, the Hungarian PM also spoke about the importance of the rights of the Hungarian minority in Ukraine, the purpose of his upcoming trip to Poland and Austria, and the priority of securing EU funds, among others, on Monday evening.

stories.title: Magyar a kegyelmi ügyről: A fideszes fiúk előszeretettel bújnak nők szoknyája mögé
stories.summary: Magyar Péter bejelentette, hogy kedden hozzák nyilvánosságra a kegyelmi ügy dossziéját. Ebben nincs benne, hogy Varga Judit miért írta alá a megváltoztatott döntést. A miniszterelnök szerint a fő felelős az, aki rávette Novák Katalint, hogy adjon kegyelmet.

stories.title: Kapu Tibor: Magyarország kutatóűrhajósa vagyok, nem a Fidesz űrhajósa, és nem is leszek a Tisza űrhajósa
stories.summary: Kapu szerint egy magyar űrhajósnak nem szabad pártpolitizálnia.

stories.title: Hétfő esti Magyar Közlöny: jönnek a soron kívüli vizsgálatok, szűkös határidőket kaptak a miniszterek
stories.summary: A dokumentumban a közigazgatási államtitkárok kinevezése mellett számos olyan ügy is szerepel, amikről a kormányülés utáni sajtótájékoztatón is szó volt.

stories.title: Reuters: Az amerikai külügyminiszter-helyettes intézte el a vízumot Ziobrónak
stories.summary: A korrupcióval gyanúsított lengyel exminiszter így még Magyar Péter beiktatása előtt el tudta hagyni Magyarországot.

stories.title: BL-sztorik: síri csend volt a lidérces döntő után a Liverpool öltözőjében, de még a repülőn is hazafelé
stories.summary: Május 30-án a sorozat történetében először rendezheti meg Budapest a Bajnokok Ligája döntőjét, a Puskás Arénában ünnepelhet Európa legjobb futballcsapata. A fináléig mindennap visszatekintünk a döntők egy-egy nagy pillanatára, egyéniségére, érdekességére. BL-sztorik, tizenharmadik rész.

stories.title: Havasi Bertalan feljelentette Magyar Pétert
stories.summary: „A kormány a reménytelen helyzetben lévő maffiatagok kétségbeesett próbálkozásával nem foglalkozik” – reagált a Telexnek a miniszterelnök.

stories.title: Bayer Zsolt: Ne tagadjuk, padlón vagyunk
stories.summary: A Fidesz megmondóembere vasárnap több műsorban is elmondta, olyan érzése van, mintha 1919 lenne.

stories.title: Perceken belül megjött a válasz Magyar Pétertől Havasi Bertalan feljelentésére
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök egyetlen mondatban intézte el az ügyet.

stories.title: Hungary and Ukraine to launch expert-level consultations on rights of Hungarians in Transcarpathia
stories.summary: Both countries' foreign ministers announced the decision on social media, with Ukraine's Sybiha saying that the aim is "to restore trust and good-neighbourly relations between our countries.”

stories.title: A hetes cikkes eljárást nem azért indítottuk, mert Orbán Viktor volt hatalmon
stories.summary: Októberben európai parlamenti küldöttség jönne Magyarországra, utána kezdeményezhetik a befagyasztott uniós pénzektől függetlenül futó hetes cikkes eljárás lezárását, mondta utóbbi jelentéstevője, Tineke Strik EP-képviselő.

stories.title: Mága Zoltán elismerte a Telexnek, hogy diplomata-útlevéllel utazott külföldi fellépésekre
stories.summary: A közpénzmilliárdokkal megtámogatott prímás olyan eseményre is így utazott, amelyen a magyar küldöttség más zenészei egyszerű útlevéllel vettek részt.

stories.title: Russian ambassador felt effect of Hungarian government change within a single day
stories.summary: The drastic shift in Hungarian diplomacy is clearly visible in Anita Orbán's summoning of the Russian ambassador over the Russian attack on Transcarpathia, just a day after she took office. Her predecessor, Péter Szijjártó regularly summoned ambassadors of Hungary's allies, but failed to summon the Russian ambassador since the war in Ukraine broke out—and even before that, he had only done so very rarely.

stories.title: Hétfőn kinevezik a közigazgatási államtitkárokat, közölte a Tisza Párt, miután Gulyás Gergely a szakmai irányítás hiányáról írt
stories.summary: Múlt héten a köztársasági elnök Magyar Péter javaslatára azonnali hatállyal felmentette az Orbán-kormány minisztériumainak közigazgatási államtitkárait.

stories.title: A thaiföldi cunami előtt elhallgattak a madarak
stories.summary: Tara Menon könyve a 2004-es szökőár történetét dolgozza fel, egyszerre állítva emléket a 220 ezer áldozatnak és azoknak, akiknek tovább kellett élniük a természeti katasztrófa után. Kritika.

stories.title: Magyar Péter nagy menete – sajtótájékoztató és elemzés
stories.summary: A kormányülés után maratoni sajtótájékoztatót tartott, holnap pedig irány Varsó, Magyar Péter első külföldi útja miniszterelnökként. Feljelentések, a kegyelmi ügy újabb fejezete, csata Havasi Bertalannal és Sulyok Tamással. De mire készül Orbán, aki nem nyúl, hanem tigris akar lenni? Élőben közvetítettük és elemeztük a sajtótájékoztatót.

stories.title: Magyar Péter: Kedden hozzák nyilvánosságra a kegyelmi ügy dossziéját, Varga Judit első körben nem támogatta K. Endre kérvényét
stories.summary: A leköszönő kormány meghamisította a költségvetést, legalább 286 milliárd forintot nem tüntetett fel – hangzott el a hétfői kormányülés után. Átalakítják a végrehajtói rendszert. Középtávon megszűnnek a vármegyék. Krausz Ferenccel hajlandóak tárgyalni, Balásy Gyulával nem. Még nem döntöttél el, mi lesz a kekvák sorsa. Az önkormányzatoknak egyelőre ki kell fizetniük a szolidaritási hozzájárulást.

stories.title: Így szólt a rendőröknek Magyar Péter, hogy Havasi Bertalan ne tartózkodjon a minisztériumban
stories.summary: Videón mutatjuk ahogy a miniszterelnök megkérdezte, van-e valaki a minisztériumból, aki megkérné Havasit, hogy fáradjon ki az épületből.

stories.title: Young Czechs drink less beer but risk other addictions
stories.summary: The Czech Republic, the beer world champion, has seen a record decline in consumption. Younger generations are drinking less. Breweries are worried, but health experts say it's good news.

stories.title: Could Romania and Moldova reunite?
stories.summary: Moldovan leader Maia Sandu and her Romanian counterpart Nicusor Dan want to reunite their countries. Is that a realistic scenario?

stories.title: Latvia: Prime Minister Evika Silina resigns over handling of drone incidents after coalition ally's withdrawal
stories.summary: Evika Silina says she is resigning her post, following the defense minister's resignation and the coalition government's collapse amid controversy over Ukrainian drones aimed at Russia that landed in the Baltic country.

stories.title: Australia news live: Karl Stefanovic and Eddie McGuire sign to co-host weekly radio show; diphtheria outbreak spreads to three states
stories.summary: The new format, The Long Weekend, will broadcast live on ARN’s Gold network. Follow today’s news live Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Global Sumud Flotilla participants urge Australian government to intervene and ‘keep us safe’ The Australians that the Global Sumud Flotilla allege have been kidnapped by the Israel Defense Forces (IDF) are academics, doctors, students, activists and film-makers. This is now the second time Israel has illegally abducted Australian citi

stories.title: Andalusia wipeout spells trouble for Spain’s Sánchez in 2027 election
stories.summary: MADRID — Spain’s flurry of regional elections over recent months — most crucially in Andalusia this Sunday — has delivered an ominous message to the ruling Socialist Party of Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez ahead of next year’s general election. As Spain’s most populous region, Andalusia was of particular importance, and the Socialists’ heavy defeat was a historic and humiliating blow for a party that once regarded it as one of its strongholds. The conservative People’

stories.title: Jury rejects Musk’s claims against Sam Altman over OpenAI Founding
stories.summary: A federal jury in California took just a few hours on Monday to unanimously dismiss Elon Musk’s claims alleging that Sam Altman and OpenAI  deceived him into funding  the company with promises to operate it as a nonprofit for the public welfare. Musk accused the ChatGPT-maker and its leaders, including CEO Altman and president Greg Brockman, of effectively “stealing a charity” by later starting a massive commercial venture that has become one of the most well known and highly valued ar

stories.title: EU looks to cow manure to keep food prices down
stories.summary: Brussels’ answer to a looming fertilizer crisis is to make more use of cow dung. Grocery price spikes are on the horizon amid the unending war in Iran and the rising cost of fertilizer. Yet the European Commission’s plan to shore up Europe’s supply, due out Tuesday, centers around a long-term regulatory push to recycle more manure and farm waste into fertilizer.  It’s not the quick fix some were hoping for. Farmers “expected bold action,” said MEP Veronika Vrecionová,

stories.title: Congo Ebola outbreak: Aid cuts and war complicate response
stories.summary: Deep foreign aid cuts allowed the deadly Ebola virus to spread undetected in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda, while ongoing conflict has complicated efforts to build resilient health care services in the region, experts warned Monday. Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez framed the crisis as a matter of security and justice at the World Health Organization’s annual meeting in Geneva, where the pressing question for health leaders is how to continue funding global health amid cu

stories.title: Yes, Trump might really attack Cuba
stories.summary: The Trump administration has been dancing around the question of whether it will carry out military strikes against Cuba. I am told it is increasingly willing to take such a step. That’s a significant escalation from a few months ago, when officials were primarily focused on using economic and diplomatic pressure to squeeze the communist regime in Havana. A U.S. official and a person familiar with the administration’s discussions on Cuba told me that President Donald Trump and his aides have gro

stories.title: Angela Merkel says Europe ‘not making sufficient use of diplomatic potential’ to engage with Russia – as it happened
stories.summary: The former German chancellor added that diplomacy alongside military deterrence is crucial when dealing with Russia. Elsewhere, we are getting a line from Rome, with the Italian foreign ministry confirming that rescuers have located the bodies of four Italian divers believed to be deep inside an underwater cave in a Maldive atoll, AP reported. Five Italian divers are believed to have died while exploring a cave at a depth of about 50 meters in Vaavu Atoll on Thursday, according to Italy’s foreig

stories.title: ‘I am not going to walk away’, says Starmer as Burnham pitches debate on ’how politics needs to change’ – as it happened
stories.summary: The prime minister said he wouldn’t be setting out a timetable to stand down if Andy Burnham wins the Makerfield byelection Jakub Krupa writes the Guardian’s Europe live blog. The European Commission has been asked about its response to the renewed talk about Britain’s potential future attempt to rejoin the European Union – but did not take a bait. At this stage, there are discussions on closer cooperation in a number of areas, that’s where we are, and that’s what we are doing precisely in prepa

stories.title: Pressure on Mexico after two ex-officials surrender to US over alleged cartel ties
stories.summary: Mexican president Claudia Sheinbaum denies any links between her Morena party and organized crime Pressure is mounting on Claudia Sheinbaum, Mexico’s president, after two former top officials from the country’s Sinaloa state – both members of her Morena party – gave themselves up to US authorities over alleged ties to the Sinaloa cartel. The state’s former security minister Gerardo Mérida Sánchez crossed the border into Arizona last week and was taken into custody by US marshals, Mexico’s securi

stories.title: Trump claims planned attack on Iran postponed after Tehran makes new proposal to end war
stories.summary: The US president says plans for a US military strike on Iran have been paused because ‘serious negotiations are now taking place’ Iran has made a new proposal for a deal to definitively end the war in the Middle East, officials in the region said on Monday, with Donald Trump claiming he had postponed new military strikes so talks could continue. But while the US president has regularly used social media to threaten Tehran, and to claim that a peace deal was within reach, there has been no sign o

stories.title: Mark Fuhrman, LA detective convicted of lying at OJ Simpson trial, dies
stories.summary: Fuhrman, who found bloody glove at Simpson’s home, charged with perjury after audio revealed use of racial slurs Former Los Angeles police detective Mark Fuhrman, who was convicted of lying during testimony at the OJ Simpson murder trial, has died. Fuhrman was one of the first two police detectives sent to investigate the 1994 killings of Simpson’s ex-wife Nicole Brown Simpson and her friend, Ronald Goldman, in Los Angeles. He reported finding a bloody glove at Simpson’s home, but his credibilit

stories.title: From sanctioned cars to beauty clinics, Russian rubles have flowed into China’s border towns since Ukraine war
stories.summary: Suifenhe, a small city in China’s economically depressed rust belt, is a microcosm of an evolving Chinese-Russian trading relationship Suited and booted in a navy twinset tracksuit and colourful high-top trainers, Wang Runguo is hustling. Darting across the gleaming floors of his cavernous car showroom, the 45-year-old from one of China’s poorest provinces is closing on yet another deal. It is all in a day’s work for the man whose salary has more than doubled in the past year thanks to a well-ti

stories.title: UAE restores power to Barakah nuclear plant after drone strike, says IAEA – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed. See our latest full report: Trump claims planned attack on Iran postponed after Tehran makes new proposal to end war Friedrich Merz has been embroiled in a row with Donald Trump over his war on Iran ever since the German chancellor suggested the Trump team was being outplayed in its negotiations with Tehran and said he would not advise his children to study or work in the US in the current climate. The Guardian’s Berlin correspondent, Deborah Cole, has looked at the decl

stories.title: UAE blames Iran or proxies for strike near nuclear plant, as Trump tells Tehran ‘clock is ticking’
stories.summary: Abu Dhabi denounces ‘dangerous escalation’ as Iran war ceasefire grows more precarious, and US president voices impatience at stalemate The United Arab Emirates has blamed a fire near its nuclear power plant on a drone launched by Iran or one of its proxies in what the UAE called a “dangerous escalation”. The fire was just outside the Barakah nuclear plant and caused no injuries or radiation alerts, with the emirate’s nuclear regulator saying there was no radioactive leak or risk to the public.

stories.title: Cuba warns US of ‘bloodbath’ if military action follows drone claims
stories.summary: Cuba’s president, Miguel Díaz-Canel, says any US strike would be catastrophic after reports of 300+ drones Cuba’s president, Miguel Díaz-Canel, has warned that any US military action against his country would lead to a “bloodbath” with incalculable consequences for regional peace and stability. “Cuba does not represent a threat,” Díaz-Canel said in a post on X. Continue reading...

stories.title: Trump delays major strikes on Iran at request of Middle East leaders
stories.summary: President Donald Trump announced Monday he would delay his plan to strike Iran in hopes of further diplomatic negotiations. The U.S. plan to further strike Iran was scheduled for Tuesday, Trump  wrote on Truth Social . He met with his national security team over the weekend after no movement on a deal with Iran, and  threatened on Sunday  that Iran “better get moving, FAST, or there won’t be anything left of them.” The president said he reconsidered the strikes after requests made

stories.title: Ex-commando Al Carns ‘keeping options open’ for challenge to Keir Starmer
stories.summary: LONDON — A little-known British defense minister is being urged by MPs to consider a bid to replace Keir Starmer, as a leadership crisis consumes the governing Labour Party. Al Carns, the minister for the armed forces, is being touted by supporters who say the party now needs a radical change if it is to win back thousands of voters who dumped Labour for Nigel Farage’s Reform UK or the left-wing Greens at recent local elections. Carns, who only entered parliament as the MP for the Birmingh

stories.title: 'Atlas of Civil Society' downgrades Germany
stories.summary: Germany is no longer considered a fully open society amid concerns over disinformation and police brutality. A 'Brot für die Welt' report highlighted migration, the LGBT+ community and climate change as being targeted.

stories.title: Could the UK really rejoin the EU? - The Latest
stories.summary: The Brexit debate has been reignited after Labour leadership contender Wes Streeting called it a ‘catastrophic mistake’ and said the UK should rejoin the European Union. His comments put pressure on rival Andy Burnham, who has previously advocated for rejoining the bloc but is fighting a byelection in the leave-voting Makerfield constituency. But how would rejoining work and would the EU even agree to it? Lucy Hough speaks to the Guardian’s Europe correspondent, Jon Henley Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘Disposable’ operatives for hire are a new menace for western countries
stories.summary: A court case in New York has highlighted how Iran is using technology to recruit agents who may not even be regime supporters When on Friday a 32-year-old Iraqi was brought before a court in New York to be charged with planning to attack Jewish community sites in the US, a curtain was suddenly lifted on a corner of a shadowy world. The detention of Mohammed Saad Baqer al-Saadi in Turkey last week revealed rare details of Iran’s efforts to use terrorism to sow discord among communities in Europe,

stories.title: ‘Dodgy’ shops handling criminal cash targeted by new specialist unit
stories.summary: Estimated £1bn per year is laundered through vape stores, barbers, mini-marts and sweet shops, officials say “Dodgy” retail outlets such as vape stores, barbers, mini-marts and sweet shops suspected of being used to launder £1bn of criminal money will be targeted by a new specialist unit, the government has said. A £20m National Crime Agency cell will run and coordinate investigations and raids into UK businesses suspected of acting as fronts for gangsters, the Home Office said. Continue reading

stories.title: Third of university students in Great Britain think AI job losses will cause social unrest, poll finds
stories.summary: Tracker of attitudes towards artificial intelligence also finds almost half of the public would prefer to avoid it One in three university students think AI will wipe out jobs so rapidly it will trigger civil unrest, according to a survey by King’s College London (KCL). Students are among the heaviest users of AI, the poll found, with 77% using it at least a few times a month – compared with 46% of workers – and 27% using it daily or almost daily. Continue reading...

stories.title: Racist abuse of NHS nurses rising amid ‘normalisation’ of extreme views, RCN warns
stories.summary: Figures disclosed by nursing union show big rise on reported incidents which may only be ‘tip of the iceberg’ Racist abuse of NHS nurses has jumped by 86% in the last few years, which their union’s boss has blamed on the normalisation of extreme views in politics and the media. One nurse was called a monkey by a colleague, a patient threw a hot drink at a nurse and followed up with racial abuse, and in several cases others were called the N-word, the Royal College of Nursing (RCN) disclosed. Con

stories.title: Trump’s shifting remarks on Taiwan are perfect for China to exploit
stories.summary: President’s remarks about Taiwan arms deals being a ‘negotiating chip’ with Beijing have been seized on by Chinese state media It has been an unsettling few days for Taiwan’s government. When Donald Trump met Xi Jinping in Beijing on Thursday, many feared the unpredictable US leader could upend Washington’s longstanding support for Taipei. But beyond a starkly worded statement from Xi stressing China’s claims over Taiwan, which it claims as part of its territory despite never having ruled it, in

stories.title: Experts locate bodies of four missing Italian divers inside Maldives cave
stories.summary: Spokesperson for Indian Ocean island nation says they will try to recover explorers in next couple of days Rescuers have located the bodies of four Italian divers deep inside an underwater cave in an atoll in the Maldives, four days after they were reported missing. Searches had resumed after being suspended following the death of a local military diver during a perilous mission to try to reach them. Continue reading...

stories.title: Tributes paid as ‘outstanding’ soldier who died in fall at Royal Windsor Horse Show is named
stories.summary: Ministry of Defence names soldier who fell from her horse as Lance Bombardier Ciara Sullivan, 24, part of the King’s Troop, Royal Horse Artillery A soldier who died after falling from her horse at the Royal Windsor Horse Show was named as Lance Bombardier Ciara Sullivan, 24 – an “exceptional jockey” with an “infectious energy”, her commanding officer said. Sullivan, part of the King’s Troop, Royal Horse Artillery, fell at around 7pm on Friday after exiting the arena. Continue reading...

stories.title: Donald Trump says he ‘wasn’t involved’ in creation of $1.7bn compensation fund – as it happened
stories.summary: This live blog is now closed. Trump dismisses $10bn suit against IRS and creates $1.7bn ‘anti-weaponization’ fund Sign up for the Breaking News US email Democratic reactions to the news about Trump moving to withdraw his lawsuit against the IRS are coming in. Ron Wyden, a top Democrat on the Senate Finance Committee, said Trump deserved no credit for dropping the lawsuit, regardless of his reasons. “Even by his standards the move he’s trying to get away with now is a stunning act of corruption,”

stories.title: Liberal senator breaks ranks to take aim at Angus Taylor’s ‘negative’ rhetoric on immigration
stories.summary: ‘We cannot continue to blame migrants for the problems of our economy,’ backbencher Andrew McLachlan says Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A Liberal senator has broken ranks to criticise Angus Taylor’s plan to bar non-citizens from accessing welfare, warning it will create “two types of members of the community” and is “not the Australian way”. The outspoken backbencher Andrew McLachlan said migrants should not be

stories.title: Kenya fuel protests turn deadly amid Iran war disruptions
stories.summary: The African country is among many dependent on fuel imports from the Gulf. Last week, authorities in the country hiked fuel prices by 23.5%.

stories.title: Trump drops IRS lawsuit, gets billion-dollar 'victim's fund'
stories.summary: A new settlement will disburse $1.76 billion to victims of so-callled "lawfare," who were prosecuted under President Joe Biden. Critics have said the move amounts to "outright corruption."

stories.title: Trump says he paused attack on Iran at Gulf leaders' request
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said he called off a planned attack on Iran after the UAE, Saudi Arabia and Qatar requested Washington to suspend strikes.

stories.title: UAE still investigating mystery nuclear plant drone attack
stories.summary: The enigma surrounding Sunday’s unprecedented drone attack on the Barakah nuclear plant deepened on Monday as the UAE said the drone came from its western border, but it did not accuse Iran.

stories.title: Lebanese president vows to 'do the impossible' to end war with Israel as strikes continue despite ceasefire
stories.summary: On Monday, the Lebanese health ministry said the death toll from Israeli strikes since the start of the war had reached 3,020, with 211 people 18 and under and 116 healthcare workers among those killed.

stories.title: Aerial footage captures police surrounding San Diego Islamic center after shooting reports
stories.summary: Aerial footage showed a large police and emergency response outside the Islamic Center of San Diego after reports of an active shooter on Monday. Authorities said multiple law enforcement units surrounded the area, while heavily armed officers were seen positioned around the mosque complex.

stories.title: 'FedEx says your parcel has drugs': The scam that trapped an Indian comedian
stories.summary: Ankita Shrivastav is among millions of Indians who fall prey to cybercrimes every year.

stories.title: Treasury extends Russian oil sanctions waiver for another month
stories.summary: The Trump administration will extend for another month a waiver allowing the sale of Russian crude already loaded on tankers, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said Monday. The move is aimed at keeping more oil on global markets and tempering crude prices as the war in Iran, now nearing its third month, continues to choke off shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. But critics have blasted the waiver as allowing Russia to profit from elevated oil prices and enriching Moscow’s war machine. The U.S.

stories.title: Starbucks Korea CEO dismissed over ad evoking massacre of pro-democracy protesters
stories.summary: ‘Tank Day’ event causes outrage with ‘malicious mockery’ of deadly crackdown during dictatorship era The chief executive of Starbucks in South Korea has been dismissed after the company ran a promotional event using slogans that evoked a massacre of pro-democracy protesters during the country’s dictatorship era, sparking outrage and boycott calls. The coffee chain launched a “Tank Day” campaign on 18 May for its “Tank” tumbler series. The date coincides with one of the most politically sensitive

stories.title: Andy Burnham’s first election hurdle: Brexit – podcast
stories.summary: Andy Burnham may already be facing a tricky obstacle in his push to return to Westminster. Past comments about rejoining the EU have come back to haunt him, particularly given that the Makerfield constituency voted overwhelmingly to leave in the Brexit referendum. Plus, Wes Streeting has confirmed he will stand in any leadership contest, raising fresh questions about where all this leaves Keir Starmer Continue reading...

stories.title: No ‘tailor-made’ deal for UK if it wants to rejoin bloc, say former EU Brexit officials
stories.summary: Britain likely to face ‘warm, welcoming stance’ if it seeks re-entry but also a ‘hard-headed one’ – with no special deals Britain would not be able to rejoin the EU on the special terms it enjoyed in the past, veterans of the Brexit negotiations have said. The warnings came as senior Labour politicians jostling for the leadership of their party and country talk openly about wanting to return to the union at some point in the future. Continue reading...

stories.title: Free up fertiliser supplies to avert global food crisis, Yvette Cooper urges
stories.summary: UK foreign secretary says urgent pressure needed to get strait of Hormuz reopened and fertiliser and fuel moving Global fertiliser supplies must be freed up within weeks to avoid disaster, with harvests suffering and food prices rising, the UK’s foreign secretary, Yvette Cooper, has said. The war in Iran has frozen shipments of fertiliser through the strait of Hormuz, creating a supply crunch that has already damaged farming in the UK , Europe and the US and is having its worst impacts in the de

stories.title: Nancy Pelosi breaks silence to endorse San Francisco supervisor as successor
stories.summary: Former House speaker, who had not yet weighed in, declared Connie Chan ‘best prepared’ to represent city Nancy Pelosi on Monday endorsed Connie Chan , a San Francisco supervisor, in the race to succeed her as the city’s representative in Congress, calling her the candidate who “stands above the rest”. Pelosi, the first woman to serve as speaker of the House, will retire at the end of her term and had not yet weighed in on the contested primary for the San Francisco district she has held for near

stories.title: The French are hitting their protein goals - thanks to a cheese that looks like ectoplasm | Emma Beddington
stories.summary: La cancoillotte, a low-fat liquid cheese product, has somehow taken the country of haute cuisine by storm Exciting news from over the Channel: a viral cheese has dropped, but good luck spelling, or saying, it. stories.title: Rejoin, reset or go Swiss-style? How the UK could try to unpick Brexit
stories.summary: Wes Streeting has suggested he wants to see the UK ‘back in the EU’ one day – but the bloc might take some convincing Is it too soon? Is it even doable? Few expected that, 10 years after the Brexit referendum and barely six years after its exit from the European Union, the UK would be talking about rejoining. stories.title: Belgian ex-diplomat dies before standing trial over 1961 murder of Congolese leader
stories.summary: Étienne Davignon, 93, was last living person targeted in investigation into assassination of DRC’s first PM, Patrice Lumumba A 93-year-old Belgian former diplomat who became the first person to be charged in the murder of the Congolese leader Patrice Lumumba has died before he could stand trial. stories.title: Spain has banned Francoist symbols. So why are there still kitsch cafes glorifying the dictator? | Abbas Asaria
stories.summary: These unsettling and unusual places tell a vivid story about the unique way that Spain deals with its past – or fails to Una Grande Libre reads the sign above the entrance to a bar-restaurant in Madrid’s Usera neighbourhood. This was Francisco Franco’s motto for Spain – one, great, free – and it is accompanied by a large portrait of the dictator superimposed on to the window. The exteriors of El Cangrejo in Ciudad Real and Casa Pepe in Despeñaperros are a little bit more subtle, but not much: de

stories.title: Weather tracker: Europe braces for swing from Arctic chill to extreme heat
stories.summary: Temperatures in France and England could rise by 15C, while hot air could to give way to snow in parts of US After a prolonged spell of cool conditions across much of Europe, a dramatic swing in temperatures is expected in the coming days as warmer air surges north into western and central parts of the continent. A large blocking high over the North Atlantic and slow-moving low pressure across southern Scandinavia dragged Arctic air southwards last week, sending temperatures 10-15C below the sea

stories.title: Shakira in line for €55m payout as Spanish court rules tax fines were wrong
stories.summary: Singer says she has faced ‘years of public shaming’, as court rules tax agency made mistake over her 2011 status A court in Madrid has ordered Spain’s tax authority to pay Shakira back more than €55m (£48m) after ruling that it had wrongly imposed huge fines on the Colombian singer and philanthropist because it had made mistakes over her tax status. stories.title: Ryanair has ‘near-zero concerns’ on fuel shortages but warns of future price rises

stories.summary: Mayor’s attempt to beautify the city with murals of mascot and plum paint jobs criticised as waste of resources The giant purple axolotl peered up at Manuel Martínez from the black bitumen of the street. It was the second such painting of the rare amphibian he had walked past that morning. In recent weeks he had seen axolotl murals pop up in neighbourhoods across Mexico City. “It’s a waste of money,” he said. “You could use that budget for fixing potholes, traffic lights, security cameras. They’

stories.title: Infectious diseases such as hantavirus and Ebola becoming more frequent and damaging, say experts

stories.title: Ebola in Africa: Why it's a constant threat

stories.title: Több száz fős leépítést jelentett be az IKEA, főleg a svéd munkavállalókat érinti
stories.summary: Az utóbbi időben csökken a fogyasztói kereslet, az iráni háború pedig tovább rontotta a helyzetet.

stories.title: Ruszin-Szendi Romulusz erős honvédelmi törvényt ígért 2027 januárjára
stories.summary: A honvédelmi miniszter szerint kidolgozzák a katonai életpálya méltó lezárásának rendszerét is.

stories.title: Bejelentette az ÁKK: csökken a két legnépszerűbb állampapír kamata
stories.summary: Egységesen változtatják a FixMÁP és a MÁP Plusz kamatszintjét.

stories.title: Nem tudta bizonyítani a spanyol adóhatóság, hogy 2011-ben adót csalt Shakira, több mint 50 millió euró visszajárhat az énekesnőnek
stories.summary: 2011-ben kevesebb mint 183 napig tartózkodott az országban a kolumbiai énekesnő, ezért kártérítés járhat neki.

stories.title: Új szintet jelent az ukrajnai korrupciós botrányban, hogy Zelenszkij egykor legbefolyásosabb embere gyanúsítottá vált
stories.summary: Andrij Jermakra már tavalyi lemondásakor is a gyanú árnyéka vetült, de eddig viszonylag aktívan jelent meg a nyilvánosság előtt, nemrég még Sean Pennt kísérte a frontvonal közelében. A hatóságok jelezték, az elnököt nem érinti a nyomozás, de Zelenszkijnek úgy kell küzdenie a korrupció ellen, hogy őt magát ne sározza be a legbelső köréig érő botrány.

stories.title: Véget ért a kormányülés, szó volt a kegyelmi dossziéról és a 2026-os költségvetésről is
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök a Facebookon írt a témákról.

stories.title: Egy négyéves, 45 milliárdos tankönyvszerződést is megörökölt az új kormány
stories.summary: Az erről szóló szerződést április 23-án kötötték meg. A közbeszerzés kiírásában azonban az elmúlt évek számaiból indultak ki, márpedig ezeken a szabad tankönyvválasztás sokat módosíthat.

stories.title: Az MBH is megjárhatja, ha tényleg elkezdődik a vagyonvisszaszerzés az MNB-botrányban
stories.summary: Kiszolgáltatott helyzetbe került a jegybanki Pallas Athéné Alapítvány az MBH Bankkal szemben.

stories.title: Dead humpback whale off Denmark is 'Timmy'
stories.summary: Divers have confirmed that a dead humpback whale spotted off Denmark is the same creature that spent weeks beached off Germany's Baltic coast. It comes two weeks after a contentious rescue operation.

stories.title: Germany news: UNICEF says child well-being ranking 'alarming'
stories.summary: A new study ranks Germany near the bottom in child well-being and education. Meanwhile, an escaped tiger was killed by police after injuring a man near Leipzig. DW has more.

stories.title: German singer Heino pushes back against the far-right AfD
stories.summary: A far‑right politician in Germany used schlager icon Heino in election advertising without his consent. Heino is now demanding compensation for pain and suffering.

stories.title: Ebola outbreak is a global health emergency, WHO says
stories.summary: The World Health Organization said that it could be a "much larger" outbreak than what is currently being identified and reported but that is does not meet the criteria of a pandemic emergency.

stories.title: Man drives car into pedestrians in Italy, injuring eight
stories.summary: Eight people were injured, four seriously, before passers-by gave chase and stopped the man.

stories.title: Eurovision winner Dara arrives to screaming fans in Bulgaria
stories.summary: The 27-year-old's tune Bangaranga won Bulgaria its first ever title in the song contest.

stories.title: World Urban Forum opens in Baku with warning on global housing divide
stories.summary: Ministers and experts opened the World Urban Forum in Baku on Sunday with stark warnings about the global housing divide, calling for international cooperation to tackle a growing crisis.

stories.title: From escaping child marriage 'to an old pervert' to becoming Sierra Leone's first lady
stories.summary: The private world of one of Africa's most powerful political women is revealed in a BBC World Service exclusive.

stories.title: Inside the 'kill-zone' on Ukraine's front line, where new weapons have transformed war
stories.summary: Machines are increasingly replacing humans on the front line, but troops are still vital for defending territory.

stories.title: 'This may be the last time you hear my voice': Political executions surge in Iran since start of war
stories.summary: Since the US and Israel attacked Iran on 28 February, the UN says it’s verified the execution of at least 32 political prisoners.

stories.title: Moment two fighter jets collide mid-air at US air show
stories.summary: Four crew members safely ejected from US Navy jets and are in a stable condition after the collision.

stories.title: Taiwan will not provoke conflict nor give up sovereignty, says president
stories.summary: Lai Ching-te made his first direct response to Trump and Xi's meeting where they discussed Taiwan independence.

stories.title: Iran has responded to latest US peace proposal, foreign ministry official says
stories.summary: On Sunday, Iran's Fars news agency said Washington had presented a five-point list, which included a demand for Iran to keep only one nuclear site in operation and transfer its stockpile of highly enriched uranium to the US.

stories.title: Trump says Islamic State ‘second in command’ killed by US and Nigerian forces
stories.summary: US president calls Abu-Bilal al-Minuki ‘most active terrorist in the world’ and says he was eliminated in ‘very complex mission’ Donald Trump has said US and Nigerian forces killed the “second in command” global leader of the Islamic State. “Tonight, at my direction, brave American forces and the Armed Forces of Nigeria flawlessly executed a meticulously planned and very complex mission to eliminate the most active terrorist in the world from the battlefield,” the US president said on his Truth

stories.title: Hezbollah drone strike videos show evolving tactics against Israel
stories.summary: BBC Verify has examined dozens of videos of fibre-optic drone attacks carried out by the Lebanese armed group.

stories.title: Arrest of Iraqi terror suspect with alleged links to Iran’s Quds Force is astonishing but not surprising
stories.summary: Iran has a long history of unconventional operations, all designed to divert, distract and destabilise current or potential enemies The arrest by US authorities of an alleged Iraqi commander of an Iranian-backed militia group now accused of responsibility for 18 terrorist attacks in the UK, Europe and Canada since the beginning of the Iran war is an astonishing development – yet not the least bit surprising. According to a complaint unsealed on Friday in a federal court in Manhattan, Mohammad Ba

stories.title: 'From collection to connection': How museums are redefining themselves
stories.summary: Gone are the days when museums were just places to see art and artifacts. On International Museum Day, DW looks at how museums are becoming unique spaces of community engagement and participation.

stories.title: Germany's housing crisis hits immigrants hardest
stories.summary: The German housing market crisis is deepening social inequality. Newcomers in particular are struggling to find affordable housing — with consequences for integration, education, and the labor market.

stories.title: Germany stakes claim in new space race
stories.summary: German companies of all sizes and all over the country are developing satellites and space applications. They want to be part of the lucrative industry, which is increasingly crucial for civilian and military purposes.

stories.title: A call to stop the global housing crisis: World Urban Forum, why it matters?
stories.summary: More than 40,000 participants are set to gather in Baku for the UN's World Urban Forum as UN-Habitat warns that 3 billion people lack adequate housing and slum populations could triple by 2050.

stories.title: Trump says 'most active terrorist in the world' killed in US-Nigerian operation
stories.summary: The "meticulously planned and very complex mission" eliminated Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, the Islamic State group's second in command globally, Trump said.

stories.title: Trump's independence warning prompts response from Taiwan
stories.summary: Taiwan's foreign ministry said in a statement following Trump's comments that "Beijing has no right to claim jurisdiction over Taiwan."

stories.title: Putin to visit China days after Trump's Beijing trip, Kremlin says
stories.summary: Putin and Chinese President Xi Jinping will discuss ways to deepen their countries' "comprehensive partnership and strategic cooperation," the Kremlin said.

stories.title: London on edge as Tommy Robinson backers and pro-Palestinian marchers rally
stories.summary: Tens of thousands of people joined rival London marches for Tommy Robinson and Palestine, as 4,000 officers used strict routes to avoid clashes and keep order.

stories.title: South Korea: Thousands watch robot monks march through Seoul’s historic streets
stories.summary: Four robot monks in traditional robes joined Seoul’s lantern parade ahead of Buddha’s birthday, symbolising Buddhism’s tech-savvy modernisation.

stories.title: Eurovision final in Vienna overshadowed by protests over Israel’s participation
stories.summary: Hundreds protested in Vienna against Israel’s participation in Eurovision 2026 as security was tightened around the Wiener Stadthalle before the grand final, amid tensions linked to the war in Gaza.

stories.title: Foreign tourists are falling out of love with Goa - here's why
stories.summary: Foreign visitors to Goa have nearly halved from their pre-Covid peak even as domestic tourism is booming.

stories.title: Lights, camera, Bangaranga: Highlights from Eurovision
stories.summary: Bulgarian singer Dara stormed to victory in the contest, while Ukraine broke the record for the longest note ever sung at Eurovision.

stories.title: US reportedly dropped fraud charges against Indian billionaire after he hired Trump’s lawyer
stories.summary: Gautam Adani, richest man in Asia, was accused of conspiring to pay $250m in bribes to Indian government officials The US Department of Justice is dropping its fraud charges against the Indian billionaire Gautam Adani, the richest man in Asia, after he hired a new legal team led by Donald Trump’s personal lawyer, according to new reports.
